4.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

4.gl: love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

5.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

7.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
7.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

7.gl: perpetual (also used of past time, or past and future as well)

7.dv: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

8.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
8.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

15.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
15.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

15.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast
